An Intelligent System for Academic Manuscript
Formatting and Compliance
A project report submitted in partial fulfillment of the requirements
for the award of the degree of
B. Tech.in Computer Science and Engineering
By
Aarushi Jain (2022UCS1591)
Aayushi Jain (2022UCS1606)
Aarushi Kushwaha (2022UCS1615)
Under the supervision of
Dr.Vijay Kumar Bohat
Computer Science and Engineering
Netaji Subhas University of Technology, Delhi
NETAJI SUBHAS UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
DELHI-110078
MARCH 2026
CERTIFICATE
This is to certify that the project titled An Intelligent System for Academic Manuscript
Formatting and Compliance is a bonafide record of the work done by
Aarushi Jain (2022UCS1591)
Aayushi Jain (2022UCS1606)
Aarushi Kushwaha (2022UCS1615)
under my supervision and guidance in partial fulfillment of the requirements for the award of
the degree of Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science and Engineering of the Netaji
Subhas University of Technology, DELHI-110078, during the year 2025 - 2026.
Their work is genuine and has not been submitted for the award of any other degree to the
best of my knowledge.
DATE:
Dr.Vijay Kumar Bohat
Assistant Professor
Division of Computer Science
Netaji Subhas University of Technology
1
DECLARATION
This is to certify that the work which is being hereby presented by us in this project titled
“An Intelligent System for Academic Manuscript Formatting and Compliance” in partial
fulfilment of the award of the Bachelor of Technology submitted at the Department of Computer
ScienceandEngineering,NetajiSubhasUniversityofTechnology,NewDelhi,isagenuine
accountofourworkcarriedoutduringtheperiodfromDecember2025toFebruary2026
undertheguidanceofDr.VijayKumarBohat,DepartmentofComputerScience,Netaji
Subhas University of Technology, New Delhi.
The matter embodied in the project report to the best of our knowledge has not been sub-
mitted for the award of any other degree elsewhere.
DATE:
Aarushi JainAayushi JainAarushi Kushwaha
(2022UCS1591)(2022UCS1606)(2022UCS1615)
2
ACKNOWLEDGEMENT
WewouldliketoexpressoursinceregratitudetoDr.VijayKumarBohat,Departmentof
Computer Science and Engineering, Netaji Subhas University of Technology, Delhi, for his in-
valuable guidance and support throughout the brainstorming, theoretical framework, and partial
implementation of this project.His insights, encouragement, and constructive feedback greatly
enhanced our understanding and helped shape the direction of this work.
We are also thankful for the environment of learning and collaboration provided by the de-
partment, which allowed us to explore ideas freely and translate them into practical outcomes.
3
Table Of Contents
CERTIFICATE......................................2
DECLARATION......................................3
ACKNOWLEDGEMENT.................................3
TABLE OF CONTENTS.................................4
INTRODUCTION.....................................5
MOTIVATION.......................................6
LITERATURE REVIEW.................................7
PROBLEM STATEMENT................................9
OBJECTIVES.......................................10
SIMULATION PLATFORM12...............................
PROPOSED METHOD..................................16
CONCLUSION......................................19
REFERENCES .......................................21
4
Introduction
FixmyPaperisaweb-basedintelligentacademicformattingverificationplatformthatisde-
signedtoautomaticallyanalyzeresearchpapersanddetectstructural,syntactic,citation,and
formatting-related errors. Academic writing in technical domains follows strict formatting stan-
dards prescribed by institutions and journals like IEEE, APA etc.These standards ensure clar-
ity, reproducibility, and professional presentation of research work.
However,undergraduatestudentsoftenlackformaltraininginacademicwritingconven-
tions.Whiletheymaypossessstrongtechnicalknowledge,theyfrequentlymakeformatting
mistakes such as:
•Improper ordering of figures and tables
•Missing figure/table captions
•Equations without numbering or explanation
•Incorrect citation styles
•Mismatched in-text citations and reference lists
•Inconsistent typography and notation
Mostofthewritingtoolsthatcurrentlyexistprimarilyfocusongrammarandlanguage
improvement, offering little support for technical formatting compliance.Fix my Paper helps
bridge this gap by integrating rule-based validation and machine learning techniques to generate
structured, actionable improvement reports within seconds.
5
Motivation
Themotivationfordevelopingourprojectarisesfromthegrowingimportanceofacademic
writingqualityintechnicaleducation.Studentswithtechnicalbackgroundsareincreasingly
required to produce research papers, project reports, and theses that adhere to strict formatting
standardsdefinedbyacademicinstitutionsandjournals.Whilestudentsoftenpossessstrong
technical knowledge,they frequently lack adequate training in academic writing conventions,
resulting in structural and formatting inconsistencies that negatively affect the clarity and pro-
fessionalism of their work.
A key motivating factor is the limited time available for faculty members to provide detailed
formatting feedback.Professors must manage multiple responsibilities, including teaching, re-
search, and administrative duties, which restricts their ability to thoroughly review formatting
aspectsofeverystudentsubmission.Asaresult,manyformattingerrorspersistacrosssub-
missions, slowing students’ development of academic writing skills and increasing the overall
review burden.
Additionally, existing writing support tools primarily focus on grammar correction and lan-
guageenhancement,offeringminimalassistancefortechnicalformattingissues.Toolsthat
do address citation management often operate independently and do not verify contextual cor-
rectnesswithinthedocument.Thisfragmentedsupportcreatesagapinautomatedacademic
quality assurance for technical papers.
The need for an affordable and student-friendly automated solution further strengthens the
motivation behind this project.Many available platforms are subscription-based and may not
beaccessibletoallstudents.Therefore,alightweightandspecializedplatformcapableof
delivering fast,actionable feedback on technical formatting can significantly improve student
productivity, reduce faculty workload, and enhance overall research paper quality.
6
Literature Review
Thedomainofacademicwritingassistancehasbeenwidelyexploredthroughvarioustools
and research efforts aimed at improving the quality of scholarly documents.A significant por-
tion of existing solutions focus on grammar correction and language refinement, helping users
enhance sentence structure, vocabulary, and readability. While these tools are effective for gen-
eral writingimprovement,they provide limited support for technical formatting requirements
specific to research papers.
Plagiarismdetectionsystemsrepresentanothercategoryofrelatedsolutions.Theseplat-
forms are primarily designed to verify the originality of the content by comparing documents
againstlargeacademicdatabases.Althoughtheyplayanessentialroleinmaintainingaca-
demic integrity, they do not address formatting correctness, structural consistency, or citation-
referencemappingwithinadocument.Consequently,plagiarismtoolsalonecannotensure
comprehensive academic formatting compliance.
Citation management software help in assisting researchers in creating and managing cita-
tions in multiple styles. However, these tools typically operate as standalone utilities and do not
verifywhetherin-textcitationsareproperlylinkedtothereferencelistorwhetherreferences
arecompleteandcorrectlyordered.Moreover,theylackthecapabilitytodetectcontextual
errorssuchasmissingcitations,incorrectbracketpunctuation,ormismatchedcitationstyles
across a document.
RecentresearchinNaturalLanguageProcessinganddocumentanalysishasintroduced
techniques for automated document segmentation, citation extraction, and metadata classifica-
tion.Machine learning models have been applied to identify reference structures and improve
academicindexingsystems.Despitetheseadvances,mostoftheresearchfocusesonlarge-
scale academic database management rather than providing practical tools at the student-level
7
for formatting verification and improvement.
The review of the existing literature highlights a clear gap in integrated solutions that com-
bine structural validation, citation verification, and technical formatting analysis within a single
platform.There is a lack of lightweight, affordable systems specifically designed for technical
research papers that can detect formatting inconsistencies related to figures, tables, equations,
andreferenceswhileofferingactionableimprovementsuggestions.Theproposedplatform
addressesthisgapbyintegratingrule-basedvalidationwithmachinelearningtechniquesto
provide comprehensive formatting assistance tailored to student research needs.
8
Problem Statement
Undergraduateandpostgraduatestudentsintechnicaldisciplinesarerequiredtopreparere-
searchpapersandprojectreportsthatfollowstrictacademicformattingstandardsprescribed
byuniversities,conferences,andjournals.However,manystudentslackformaltrainingin
technical writing and formatting conventions, which results in frequent structural and stylistic
errors in their submissions.These errors include improper figure and table referencing,miss-
ing or incorrectly placed captions, unnumbered or unexplained equations, inconsistent citation
styles, and mismatches between in-text citations and the reference list.
The absence of timely and detailed feedback further aggravates the problem.Faculty mem-
bers often face a heavy workload and are unable to provide comprehensive formatting correc-
tions for every submission.As a result, students repeatedly make similar mistakes, leading to
poor document quality, increased review cycles, and delays in evaluation or publication.
Existingwritingassistancetoolsprimarilyfocusongrammarandlanguageimprovement
andofferlimitedsupportfortechnicalformattingverification.Toolsthathandlecitationsor
referencesworkinisolationanddonotvalidatetheoverallstructuralconsistencyofthedoc-
ument.Consequently,thereisalackofanintegrated,automatedsolutionthatcananalyze
multiplecomponentsofatechnicalpaper,includingtextstructure,figures,tables,equations,
and references.
Therefore,thereisaneedforacomprehensiveandautomatedsystemthatcanaccurately
detectformattinginconsistencies,structuralerrors,andreference-relatedissuesintechnical
academic documents and provide actionable feedback to help students improve the quality and
compliance of their research papers.
9
Objectives
The primary objective of our project is to develop an intelligent, automated system capable of
verifying the structural and formatting correctness of technical research papers. The platform is
designed to go beyond traditional grammar-checking tools by focusing specifically on academic
formatting standards required in engineering and scientific writing.The purpose of this project
is to ensure that research documents comply with institutional and journal-specific guidelines
prior to submission.
Figure 1:System Architecture of Fix my Paper
Oneofthekeyobjectivesistoautomaticallydetectformattinginconsistenciesrelatedto
figures, tables, and equations.This includes verifying whether figures and tables are properly
numbered,correctlyreferencedinthetext,placedaftertheirfirstmention,andaccompanied
byappropriatecaptions.Formathematicalexpressions,thesystemaimstochecksequential
equationnumbering,properformattingofvariablesandunits,andwhetherallsymbolsare
defined clearly within the text.
10
Another important objective is to validate citation and reference integrity. The system seeks
toensurethatin-textcitationscorrectlycorrespondtoentriesinthereferencelist,thatrefer-
encesarecompleteandproperlyordered,andthatcitationstylessuchasIEEEorAPAare
consistently applied.By automating these checks, the platform reduces the likelihood of sub-
mission rejections due to referencing errors.
The project also aims to reduce manual correction effort for both students and faculty mem-
bers.By generating structured and actionable feedback reports within a short processing time,
thesystemencouragesstudentstoindependentlyrefinetheirworkbeforesubmission.This
objectivedirectlycontributestoimprovingoverallacademicwritingqualityandpromoting
self-learning.
Fromamethodologicalperspective,thesystemfollowsastructuredprocessingpipeline.
The process begins when a user uploads a research paper in PDF or DOC format.The backend
then extracts textual content, figures, tables, equations, and references using document parsing
utilities.
Following extraction, a rule-based engine employing regular expressions and pattern-matching
techniques analyzes structural formatting aspects. These rules are designed to detect errors such
as skipped numbering sequences, incorrect caption placement, improper punctuation, inconsis-
tent typography, and citation bracket formatting issues.
Inparallel,machinelearningmodelsareappliedtoanalyzethereferencessectionmore
intelligently.Thesemodelsassistinidentifyingincompletereferences,mismatchesbetween
in-text citations and bibliography entries, DOI validation issues, and inconsistencies in citation
styleformatting.Thecombinationofrule-basedlogicandmachinelearningenhancesboth
accuracy and contextual understanding.
Finally, the detected issues are aggregated into a structured analysis report.The report cat-
egorizes errors by severity and type, provides specific suggestions for correction, and may in-
clude an overall formatting compliance score. This report is then displayed through an intuitive
web interface, allowing users to easily review and implement the suggested improvements.
Through this systematic objective-driven methodology,our project aims to deliver a com-
prehensive, efficient, and user-friendly solution for technical academic formatting verification.
11
Simulation Platform and Requirements
Thesimulationplatformfortheprojectisdesignedtoreplicateareal-worldacademicdocu-
ment processing environment where research papers are uploaded, analyzed, and evaluated for
formatting compliance.
The validation checks implemented in the simulation environment are listed in Table 1.
Table 1:Stage-1 Validation Checks Used in Simulation
#CategoryCheckable Requirement
1Figures & TablesEvery figure and table is cited in the main text
2Figures & TablesNo figure or table appears without textual reference
3FiguresFigure captions are placed below the figure
4TablesTable captions are placed above the table
5Figures & TablesFiguresandtablesarenumberedsequentially(no
skipping)
6Figures & TablesFigures and tables appear after first mention in text
7TablesUnits are specified in all column headers
8Figures & TablesImages are clear, readable, and high resolution
9EquationsAll equations are numbered consecutively
10EquationsEquation numbers are in parentheses and flush right
11EquationsEvery equation is referenced in the text
12EquationsAllsymbolsaredefinedbeforeorimmediatelyafter
use
13Math FormattingVariables are italicized
14Math FormattingFunctions and units are in roman (non-italic) font
12
#CategoryCheckable Requirement
15Math FormattingProper punctuation is used after equations
16CitationsCitationstylematchesthetargetjournal(IEEE/A-
PA/etc.)
17CitationsBracket punctuation is correct (e.g., [1], not .[1])
18CitationsOne-to-one match between in-text citations and refer-
ence list
19ReferencesNo missing or uncited references
20ReferencesReferences are ordered correctly
21ReferencesReferencesarecomplete(authors,title,venue,year,
pages)
22ReferencesDOI included wherever available
23ReferencesJournal title casing is consistent
24ReferencesURLs and DOIs are valid and unbroken
25ReferencesRecentreferencesincluded(last5yearswhererele-
vant)
26AcronymsAcronyms are defined at first occurrence
27UnitsNon-breakingspacebetweennumberandunit(e.g.,
10 kg)
28TypographyEn-dash used for numeric ranges (e.g., 10–20)
29TypographyConsistent spacing after commas and full stops
30ConsistencyNotation, symbols, and terminology used consistently
ThefrontendoftheplatformisbuiltusingmodernwebtechnologiessuchasReact.jsor
Next.js, combined with CSS for responsive and clean user interface design.The purpose of the
frontend is to provide a smooth document upload experience, display structured error reports,
and ensure intuitive navigation.The interface is designed to clearly categorize detected issues
under sections such as figures, equations, citations, references, and typography, making it easy
for users to understand and correct errors.
13
ThebackendofthesystemisimplementedusingNode.jsorPythonwithFastAPI.The
backend handles core processing tasks, including document parsing, rule-based validation, ma-
chine learning inference, and report generation.FastAPI is particularly suitable due to its high
performance,asynchronouscapabilities,andcompatibilitywithmachinelearningworkflows.
Thebackendactsasthecentralprocessingunitoftheplatform,coordinatingdataextraction,
analysis, and structured output generation.
Fordocumentanalysisandmachinelearningoperations,wehaveusedPythonasthepri-
maryprogramminglanguageduetoitsrichecosystemoflibraries.Regularexpressionsand
rule-based engines are employed to detect formatting patterns such as figure numbering, cita-
tion brackets, equation formatting, and punctuation consistency.In addition, Natural Language
ProcessingtechniquesandScikit-learnmodelsassistinmorecomplextaskssuchascitation-
reference matching,reference completeness verification, and DOI validation.This hybrid ap-
proach ensures both speed and contextual intelligence in error detection.
The system requires a reliable database layer, such as PostgreSQL or MongoDB, to manage
user session data, analysis results, and system logs temporarily.While uploaded documents are
not stored permanently in order to maintain privacy, temporary storage ensures smooth report
generationandretrieval.Datadeletionpoliciesareimplementedtoremovedocumentsafter
processing, ensuring compliance with privacy and academic integrity standards.
From an infrastructure perspective,the platform is deployed on a cloud-based virtual ma-
chine capable of handling document uploads and concurrent processing requests. Containeriza-
tion using Docker ensures portability and consistent deployment across environments.Object
storage is utilized for temporary file management, while monitoring and logging services main-
tain system reliability, uptime tracking, and performance diagnostics.
For machine learning model training and experimentation, a short-term GPU-enabled com-
pute instance may be required.This is particularly useful during development and optimization
phasestoimprovemodelaccuracyinreferenceclassificationandpatternrecognition.Once
trained, inference can be performed efficiently on standard CPU-based servers to reduce oper-
ational costs.
Security and accessibility are also essential requirements of the platform.A registered do-
14
main name and SSL certificate are necessary to ensure secure HTTPS communication between
usersandtheserver.Authenticationmechanismscanbeintegratedinlaterstagestosupport
institutional adoption and personalized report tracking.
Overall, the simulation platform integrates modern web technologies, cloud infrastructure,
and machine learning frameworks to create a scalable and efficient academic formatting veri-
fication system.The combination of frontend usability, backend computational efficiency, and
secure deployment ensures that the system can operate reliably under real-world academic us-
age conditions.
15
Proposed Method
Theproposedsystemisdesignedtoautomaticallyanalyzetechnicalresearchdocumentsand
identify structural, formatting, and reference-related issues.The methodology of the system is
described below.
1.Document Upload and Input Handling
The process begins with the user uploading a research paper through a web-based inter-
face.ThesystemacceptsdocumentsinPDForDOCformat.Onceuploaded,thefile
issecurelystoredandpassedtotheprocessingmoduleforfurtheranalysis.Theinter-
faceisdesignedtobesimpleandaccessible,allowingstudentstoquicklysubmittheir
documents for evaluation.
2.Text Extraction and Preprocessing
After the document is uploaded, the system extracts textual and structural content using
document parsing libraries.The extracted content is cleaned and organized into logical
components such as headings, paragraphs, figures, tables, equations, and references. Pre-
processing ensures that the data is structured properly so that different validation modules
can analyze each component efficiently.
3.Structural and Formatting Validation
The system applies a rule-based validation engine to identify formatting inconsistencies.
Regularexpressiontechniquesareusedtocheckfigureandtablenumbering,caption
placement,equation numbering,and citation formats.The system also verifies whether
figures,tables,andequationsareproperlyreferencedwithinthetextandwhethertheir
numbering sequence is consistent throughout the document.
16
4.Citation and Reference Analysis
Toensureaccuracyinacademicreferencing,thesystemperformscitationverification
using natural language processing techniques.It checks the relationship between in-text
citationsandthebibliographytodetectmissingreferences,duplicates,ormismatches.
Thesystemalsovalidateswhetherthecitationformatfollowsstandardstylessuchas
IEEE or APA.
5.Contextual Analysis of Technical Elements
Theproposedmethodincludesmulti-modalanalysistoevaluatefiguresandequations
incontext.Thesystemcheckswhethereachfigureorequationisaccompaniedbyan
appropriate explanation in the surrounding text.This ensures that technical elements are
not only present but are also properly integrated into the document.
6.Report Generation and Feedback
Aftercompletingtheanalysis,thesystemgeneratesadetailedreportthatcategorizes
errorsbasedontypeandseverity.Thereportincludesactionablesuggestionsforim-
provement, allowing users to correct issues before final submission.The entire analysis
process is optimized for speed and produces results within approximately 40 seconds.
7.System Benefits
The proposed system is lightweight, scalable, and cost-effective for students.It reduces
manual formatting effort and helps improve the overall quality of academic submissions.
Additionally,itminimizestheworkloadoffacultymembersbyautomatingrepetitive
formatting checks.
8.Comparison with Existing Solutions
The advantages of the proposed system over existing tools are presented in Table 2.
17
Table 2:Comparison between Fix my Paper and Existing Solutions
Fix my Paper (our solution)Existing Solutions
Advanced AI-driven analysisBasic formatting checks
Image and equation context checkLimited technical paper support
Affordable for studentsHigher cost barrier
Lightning-fast results in just 40 sec-Slower processing (up to 2 minutes)
onds
Tailored for technical papersGeneric approach to all papers
EmpowersstudentsandreducesFocusesmainlyonlanguageand
faculty workloadstyle
18
Conclusion
Our project proposes a scalable and intelligent academic formatting verification system specifi-
cally designed for technical research papers in engineering and related disciplines.Unlike con-
ventionalwritingtoolsthatfocusmainlyongrammarandlanguagerefinement,thisplatform
concentratesonstructuralaccuracy,formattingcompliance,andreferenceintegrity.Byinte-
grating rule-based validation mechanisms with machine learning models, the system is capable
of performing a detailed analysis of figures, tables, equations, citations, references, typography,
and document consistency within a single unified framework.
The combination of deterministic rule engines and data-driven models enhances both pre-
cisionandcontextualunderstanding.Rule-basedlogicensuresstrictadherencetopredefined
academicstandards,suchassequentialnumberingoffiguresandequations,correctcaption
placement,propercitationbracketformatting,andconsistentpunctuation.Atthesametime,
machinelearningmodelsprovideintelligentvalidationofreferencesectionsbyidentifying
incomplete entries, mismatches between in-text citations and bibliography items, DOI irregu-
larities,andstyleinconsistencies.Thishybridarchitectureallowsthesystemtodelivercom-
prehensive and reliable formatting verification.
One of the primary impacts of the platform is the improvement of student academic writing
quality.By receiving structured, categorized feedback before submission, students can identify
recurring formatting mistakes and correct them independently.Over time, this promotes better
understandingofacademicconventionsandstrengthenstechnicalwritingskills.Insteadof
relying solely on manual feedback from faculty members, students gain access to an automated
assistant that supports continuous learning and self-correction.
Thesystemalsosignificantlyreducesrepetitivefacultyworkload.Formattingerrorssuch
asincorrectnumbering,missingreferences,undefinedvariables,andcitationmismatchesof-
19
tenconsumesubstantialreviewtimedespitebeingnon-conceptualissues.Byautomatingthe
detection of such errors, the platform enables faculty members to focus more on evaluating the
technical depth and originality of the research rather than routine formatting corrections.This
contributes to improved efficiency in academic evaluation processes.
Anotherimportantcontributionofthesolutionisenhancedcompliancewithjournaland
institutionalstandards.Researchpapersintendedforpublicationmustadherestrictlytopre-
scribed formatting guidelines such as IEEE or APA styles.Even minor inconsistencies can re-
sult in rejection or revision requests.By verifying citation styles, reference ordering, structural
consistency,and typographic conventions,the system increases the likelihood of submissions
meeting professional standards on the first attempt.
Affordability and accessibility are also central to the platform’s design philosophy.Many
existing academic support tools are expensive subscription-based services that may not be ac-
cessibletoallstudents.Thisprojectaimstoprovidefastandcost-effectiveautomatedassis-
tance,deliveringdetailedanalysisreportswithinashortprocessingtime.Thisensuresthat
students from diverse academic backgrounds can benefit from advanced formatting verification
without financial barriers.
With pilot testing, iterative refinement, and performance optimization, the system has strong
potential for institutional-level adoption across engineering colleges and technical universities.
Itcanbeintegratedintosubmissionworkflows,departmentalreviewprocesses,orresearch
labsto establisha standardizedpre-submission verification mechanism.In the long term,the
platform may evolve into a comprehensive academic quality assurance tool, supporting large-
scale document validation while promoting higher standards of technical writing excellence.
20

4
Introduction
Fix my Paper is a web-based intelligent academic formatting verification platform that is de-
signed to automatically analyze research papers and detect structural, syntactic, citation, and
formatting-related errors. Academic writing in technical domains follows strict formatting stan-
dards prescribed by institutions and journals like IEEE, APA etc. These standards ensure clar-
ity, reproducibility, and professional presentation of research work.
However, undergraduate students often lack formal training in academic writing conven-
tions. While they may possess strong technical knowledge, they frequently make formatting
mistakes such as:
• Improper ordering of figures and tables
• Missing figure/table captions
• Equations without numbering or explanation
• Incorrect citation styles
• Mismatched in-text citations and reference lists
• Inconsistent typography and notation
Most of the writing tools that currently exist primarily focus on grammar and language
improvement, offering little support for technical formatting compliance. Fix my Paper helps
bridge this gap by integrating rule-based validation and machine learning techniques to generate
structured, actionable improvement reports within seconds.
5
Motivation
The motivation for developing our project arises from the growing importance of academic
writing quality in technical education. Students with technical backgrounds are increasingly
required to produce research papers, project reports, and theses that adhere to strict formatting
standards defined by academic institutions and journals. While students often possess strong
technical knowledge, they frequently lack adequate training in academic writing conventions,
resulting in structural and formatting inconsistencies that negatively affect the clarity and pro-
fessionalism of their work.
A key motivating factor is the limited time available for faculty members to provide detailed
formatting feedback. Professors must manage multiple responsibilities, including teaching, re-
search, and administrative duties, which restricts their ability to thoroughly review formatting
aspects of every student submission. As a result, many formatting errors persist across sub-
missions, slowing students’ development of academic writing skills and increasing the overall
review burden.
Additionally, existing writing support tools primarily focus on grammar correction and lan-
guage enhancement, offering minimal assistance for technical formatting issues . Tools that
do address citation management often operate independently and do not verify contextual cor-
rectness within the document. This fragmented support creates a gap in automated academic
quality assurance for technical papers.
The need for an affordable and student-friendly automated solution further strengthens the
motivation behind this project. Many available platforms are subscription-based and may not
be accessible to all students. Therefore, a lightweight and specialized platform capable of
delivering fast, actionable feedback on technical formatting can significantly improve student
productivity, reduce faculty workload, and enhance overall research paper quality.
6
Literature Review
The domain of academic writing assistance has been widely explored through various tools
and research efforts aimed at improving the quality of scholarly documents. A significant por-
tion of existing solutions focus on grammar correction and language refinement, helping users
enhance sentence structure, vocabulary, and readability. While these tools are effective for gen-
eral writing improvement, they provide limited support for technical formatting requirements
specific to research papers.
Plagiarism detection systems represent another category of related solutions. These plat-
forms are primarily designed to verify the originality of the content by comparing documents
against large academic databases. Although they play an essential role in maintaining aca-
demic integrity, they do not address formatting correctness, structural consistency, or citation-
reference mapping within a document. Consequently, plagiarism tools alone cannot ensure
comprehensive academic formatting compliance.
Citation management software help in assisting researchers in creating and managing cita-
tions in multiple styles. However, these tools typically operate as standalone utilities and do not
verify whether in-text citations are properly linked to the reference list or whether references
are complete and correctly ordered. Moreover, they lack the capability to detect contextual
errors such as missing citations, incorrect bracket punctuation, or mismatched citation styles
across a document.
Recent research in Natural Language Processing and document analysis has introduced
techniques for automated document segmentation, citation extraction, and metadata classifica-
tion. Machine learning models have been applied to identify reference structures and improve
academic indexing systems. Despite these advances, most of the research focuses on large-
scale academic database management rather than providing practical tools at the student-level
7
for formatting verification and improvement.
The review of the existing literature highlights a clear gap in integrated solutions that com-
bine structural validation, citation verification, and technical formatting analysis within a single
platform. There is a lack of lightweight, affordable systems specifically designed for technical
research papers that can detect formatting inconsistencies related to figures, tables, equations,
and references while offering actionable improvement suggestions. The proposed platform
addresses this gap by integrating rule-based validation with machine learning techniques to
provide comprehensive formatting assistance tailored to student research needs.
8
Problem Statement
Undergraduate and postgraduate students in technical disciplines are required to prepare re-
search papers and project reports that follow strict academic formatting standards prescribed
by universities, conferences, and journals. However, many students lack formal training in
technical writing and formatting conventions, which results in frequent structural and stylistic
errors in their submissions. These errors include improper figure and table referencing, miss-
ing or incorrectly placed captions, unnumbered or unexplained equations, inconsistent citation
styles, and mismatches between in-text citations and the reference list.
The absence of timely and detailed feedback further aggravates the problem. Faculty mem-
bers often face a heavy workload and are unable to provide comprehensive formatting correc-
tions for every submission. As a result, students repeatedly make similar mistakes, leading to
poor document quality, increased review cycles, and delays in evaluation or publication.
Existing writing assistance tools primarily focus on grammar and language improvement
and offer limited support for technical formatting verification. Tools that handle citations or
references work in isolation and do not validate the overall structural consistency of the doc-
ument. Consequently, there is a lack of an integrated, automated solution that can analyze
multiple components of a technical paper, including text structure, figures, tables, equations,
and references.
Therefore, there is a need for a comprehensive and automated system that can accurately
detect formatting inconsistencies, structural errors, and reference-related issues in technical
academic documents and provide actionable feedback to help students improve the quality and
compliance of their research papers.
9
Objectives
The primary objective of our project is to develop an intelligent, automated system capable of
verifying the structural and formatting correctness of technical research papers. The platform is
designed to go beyond traditional grammar-checking tools by focusing specifically on academic
formatting standards required in engineering and scientific writing. The purpose of this project
is to ensure that research documents comply with institutional and journal-specific guidelines
prior to submission.
Figure 1: System Architecture of Fix my Paper
One of the key objectives is to automatically detect formatting inconsistencies related to
figures, tables, and equations. This includes verifying whether figures and tables are properly
numbered, correctly referenced in the text, placed after their first mention, and accompanied
by appropriate captions. For mathematical expressions, the system aims to check sequential
equation numbering, proper formatting of variables and units, and whether all symbols are
defined clearly within the text.
10
Another important objective is to validate citation and reference integrity. The system seeks
to ensure that in-text citations correctly correspond to entries in the reference list, that refer-
ences are complete and properly ordered, and that citation styles such as IEEE or APA are
consistently applied. By automating these checks, the platform reduces the likelihood of sub-
mission rejections due to referencing errors.
The project also aims to reduce manual correction effort for both students and faculty mem-
bers. By generating structured and actionable feedback reports within a short processing time,
the system encourages students to independently refine their work before submission. This
objective directly contributes to improving overall academic writing quality and promoting
self-learning.
From a methodological perspective, the system follows a structured processing pipeline.
The process begins when a user uploads a research paper in PDF or DOC format. The backend
then extracts textual content, figures, tables, equations, and references using document parsing
utilities.
Following extraction, a rule-based engine employing regular expressions and pattern-matching
techniques analyzes structural formatting aspects. These rules are designed to detect errors such
as skipped numbering sequences, incorrect caption placement, improper punctuation, inconsis-
tent typography, and citation bracket formatting issues.
In parallel, machine learning models are applied to analyze the references section more
intelligently. These models assist in identifying incomplete references, mismatches between
in-text citations and bibliography entries, DOI validation issues, and inconsistencies in citation
style formatting. The combination of rule-based logic and machine learning enhances both
accuracy and contextual understanding.
Finally, the detected issues are aggregated into a structured analysis report. The report cat-
egorizes errors by severity and type, provides specific suggestions for correction, and may in-
clude an overall formatting compliance score. This report is then displayed through an intuitive
web interface, allowing users to easily review and implement the suggested improvements.
Through this systematic objective-driven methodology, our project aims to deliver a com-
prehensive, efficient, and user-friendly solution for technical academic formatting verification.
11
Simulation Platform and Requirements
The simulation platform for the project is designed to replicate a real-world academic docu-
ment processing environment where research papers are uploaded, analyzed, and evaluated for
formatting compliance.
The validation checks implemented in the simulation environment are listed in Table 1.
Table 1: Stage-1 Validation Checks Used in Simulation
#

Checkable Requirement

2

3

4

skipping)

7

Figures & Tables

9

10

11

12

use

12

15

16

PA/etc.)
17

ence list

30

The frontend of the platform is built using modern web technologies such as React.js or
Next.js, combined with CSS for responsive and clean user interface design. The purpose of the
frontend is to provide a smooth document upload experience, display structured error reports,
and ensure intuitive navigation. The interface is designed to clearly categorize detected issues
under sections such as figures, equations, citations, references, and typography, making it easy
for users to understand and correct errors.
13
The backend of the system is implemented using Node.js or Python with FastAPI. The
backend handles core processing tasks, including document parsing, rule-based validation, ma-
chine learning inference, and report generation. FastAPI is particularly suitable due to its high
performance, asynchronous capabilities, and compatibility with machine learning workflows.
The backend acts as the central processing unit of the platform, coordinating data extraction,
analysis, and structured output generation.
For document analysis and machine learning operations, we have used Python as the pri-
mary programming language due to its rich ecosystem of libraries. Regular expressions and
rule-based engines are employed to detect formatting patterns such as figure numbering, cita-
tion brackets, equation formatting, and punctuation consistency. In addition, Natural Language
Processing techniques and Scikit-learn models assist in more complex tasks such as citation-
reference matching, reference completeness verification, and DOI validation. This hybrid ap-
proach ensures both speed and contextual intelligence in error detection.
The system requires a reliable database layer, such as PostgreSQL or MongoDB, to manage
user session data, analysis results, and system logs temporarily. While uploaded documents are
not stored permanently in order to maintain privacy, temporary storage ensures smooth report
generation and retrieval. Data deletion policies are implemented to remove documents after
processing, ensuring compliance with privacy and academic integrity standards.
From an infrastructure perspective, the platform is deployed on a cloud-based virtual ma-
chine capable of handling document uploads and concurrent processing requests. Containeriza-
tion using Docker ensures portability and consistent deployment across environments. Object
storage is utilized for temporary file management, while monitoring and logging services main-
tain system reliability, uptime tracking, and performance diagnostics.
For machine learning model training and experimentation, a short-term GPU-enabled com-
pute instance may be required. This is particularly useful during development and optimization
phases to improve model accuracy in reference classification and pattern recognition. Once
trained, inference can be performed efficiently on standard CPU-based servers to reduce oper-
ational costs.
Security and accessibility are also essential requirements of the platform. A registered do-
14
main name and SSL certificate are necessary to ensure secure HTTPS communication between
users and the server. Authentication mechanisms can be integrated in later stages to support
institutional adoption and personalized report tracking.
Overall, the simulation platform integrates modern web technologies, cloud infrastructure,
and machine learning frameworks to create a scalable and efficient academic formatting veri-
fication system. The combination of frontend usability, backend computational efficiency, and
secure deployment ensures that the system can operate reliably under real-world academic us-
age conditions.
15
Proposed Method
The proposed system is designed to automatically analyze technical research documents and
identify structural, formatting, and reference-related issues. The methodology of the system is
described below.
1. Document Upload and Input Handling
The process begins with the user uploading a research paper through a web-based inter-
face. The system accepts documents in PDF or DOC format. Once uploaded, the file
is securely stored and passed to the processing module for further analysis. The inter-
face is designed to be simple and accessible, allowing students to quickly submit their
documents for evaluation.
2. Text Extraction and Preprocessing
After the document is uploaded, the system extracts textual and structural content using
document parsing libraries. The extracted content is cleaned and organized into logical
components such as headings, paragraphs, figures, tables, equations, and references. Pre-
processing ensures that the data is structured properly so that different validation modules
can analyze each component efficiently.
3. Structural and Formatting Validation
The system applies a rule-based validation engine to identify formatting inconsistencies.
Regular expression techniques are used to check figure and table numbering, caption
placement, equation numbering, and citation formats. The system also verifies whether
figures, tables, and equations are properly referenced within the text and whether their
numbering sequence is consistent throughout the document.
16
4. Citation and Reference Analysis
To ensure accuracy in academic referencing, the system performs citation verification
using natural language processing techniques. It checks the relationship between in-text
citations and the bibliography to detect missing references, duplicates, or mismatches.
The system also validates whether the citation format follows standard styles such as
IEEE or APA.
5. Contextual Analysis of Technical Elements
The proposed method includes multi-modal analysis to evaluate figures and equations
in context. The system checks whether each figure or equation is accompanied by an
appropriate explanation in the surrounding text. This ensures that technical elements are
not only present but are also properly integrated into the document.
6. Report Generation and Feedback
After completing the analysis, the system generates a detailed report that categorizes
errors based on type and severity. The report includes actionable suggestions for im-
provement, allowing users to correct issues before final submission. The entire analysis
process is optimized for speed and produces results within approximately 40 seconds.
7. System Benefits
The proposed system is lightweight, scalable, and cost-effective for students. It reduces
manual formatting effort and helps improve the overall quality of academic submissions.
Additionally, it minimizes the workload of faculty members by automating repetitive
formatting checks.
8. Comparison with Existing Solutions
The advantages of the proposed system over existing tools are presented in Table 2.
17
Table 2: Comparison between Fix my Paper and Existing Solutions
Fix my Paper (our solution)
Existing Solutions
Advanced AI-driven analysis
Basic formatting checks
Image and equation context check
Limited technical paper support
Affordable for students
Higher cost barrier
Lightning-fast results in just 40 sec-
onds
Slower processing (up to 2 minutes)
Tailored for technical papers
Generic approach to all papers
Empowers students and reduces
faculty workload
Focuses mainly on language and
style
18
Conclusion
Our project proposes a scalable and intelligent academic formatting verification system specifi-
cally designed for technical research papers in engineering and related disciplines. Unlike con-
ventional writing tools that focus mainly on grammar and language refinement, this platform
concentrates on structural accuracy, formatting compliance, and reference integrity. By inte-
grating rule-based validation mechanisms with machine learning models, the system is capable
of performing a detailed analysis of figures, tables, equations, citations, references, typography,
and document consistency within a single unified framework.
The combination of deterministic rule engines and data-driven models enhances both pre-
cision and contextual understanding. Rule-based logic ensures strict adherence to predefined
academic standards, such as sequential numbering of figures and equations, correct caption
placement, proper citation bracket formatting, and consistent punctuation. At the same time,
machine learning models provide intelligent validation of reference sections by identifying
incomplete entries, mismatches between in-text citations and bibliography items, DOI irregu-
larities, and style inconsistencies. This hybrid architecture allows the system to deliver com-
prehensive and reliable formatting verification.
One of the primary impacts of the platform is the improvement of student academic writing
quality. By receiving structured, categorized feedback before submission, students can identify
recurring formatting mistakes and correct them independently. Over time, this promotes better
understanding of academic conventions and strengthens technical writing skills. Instead of
relying solely on manual feedback from faculty members, students gain access to an automated
assistant that supports continuous learning and self-correction.
The system also significantly reduces repetitive faculty workload. Formatting errors such
as incorrect numbering, missing references, undefined variables, and citation mismatches of-
19
ten consume substantial review time despite being non-conceptual issues. By automating the
detection of such errors, the platform enables faculty members to focus more on evaluating the
technical depth and originality of the research rather than routine formatting corrections. This
contributes to improved efficiency in academic evaluation processes.
Another important contribution of the solution is enhanced compliance with journal and
institutional standards. Research papers intended for publication must adhere strictly to pre-
scribed formatting guidelines such as IEEE or APA styles. Even minor inconsistencies can re-
sult in rejection or revision requests. By verifying citation styles, reference ordering, structural
consistency, and typographic conventions, the system increases the likelihood of submissions
meeting professional standards on the first attempt.
Affordability and accessibility are also central to the platform’s design philosophy. Many
existing academic support tools are expensive subscription-based services that may not be ac-
cessible to all students. This project aims to provide fast and cost-effective automated assis-
tance, delivering detailed analysis reports within a short processing time. This ensures that
students from diverse academic backgrounds can benefit from advanced formatting verification
without financial barriers.
With pilot testing, iterative refinement, and performance optimization, the system has strong
potential for institutional-level adoption across engineering colleges and technical universities.
It can be integrated into submission workflows, departmental review processes, or research
labs to establish a standardized pre-submission verification mechanism. In the long term, the
platform may evolve into a comprehensive academic quality assurance tool, supporting large-
scale document validation while promoting higher standards of technical writing excellence.
20
